title: Robata Robata Grilled Banh Cuon with Floral Fried Vietnamese Bottarga
description: A delicate, crispy Vietnamese spring roll filled with flavorful fried bottarga, best served on a midnight craving. The combination of crunch and softness from the banh cuon and the floral notes from the bottarga creates a truly unique dining experience.

ingredients.name: Rajasic-friendly herbs (such as basil and mint)
Ayurvedic-friendly spices (such as turmeric and cumin)

instructions: Heat the sesame oil in the robata grill over medium heat.
Fry the Vietnamese bottarga until crispy, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Prepare the banh cuon by soaking the rice paper wraps in warm water for a few seconds.
Cut the crispy fried shallots into small pieces and set aside.
Chop the cilantro and mint leaves, and set aside.
Mix the chili sauce and hoisin sauce in a small bowl.

Assemble the banh cuon by placing a piece of fried bottarga in the center of a rice paper wrap, followed by a few pieces of crispy fried shallots, a sprinkle of chopped cilantro and mint leaves, and a drizzle of chili sauce and hoisin sauce mixture.
Roll the banh cuon tightly and serve immediately.
Serve the banh cuon with a side of lime juice, granulated sugar, and a sprinkle of rajasic-friendly herbs and ayurvedic-friendly spices.
Garnish with fresh mint leaves and crispy fried shallots, if desired.
